session.user:
Run these commands in sequence and report ALL failures:
1. First run: ``` cd /home/runner/work/erk/erk && uv run pytest tests/unit/ -x -q --tb=short 2>&1 | tail -40 ```
2. If that passes, run: ``` cd /home/runner/work/erk/erk && uv run pytest tests/commands/ tests/core/ tests/packages/ tests/tui/ -x -q --tb=short 2>&1 | tail -40 ```
Report the complete output of each command. Do NOT modify any files.
session.assistant: I'll run these pytest commands in sequence and report all results.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Run unit tests with short traceback

session.assistant:
pytest failed: 1 issue found
**Test:** `test_cli_requires_behind_count` (tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_handle_no_changes.py:398)
**Error:** The test expects either "Missing option" or "required" in the error output, but received "No such option: --plan-id"
**Output:** The command produced: ``` Usage: handle-no-changes [OPTIONS] Try 'handle-no-changes --help' for help.
Error: No such option: --plan-id ```
This indicates that `--plan-id` option no longer exists or has been renamed (likely as part of the "Rename Click Arguments/Options" objective #9109). The test is checking for a missing option error but using an old option name that no longer exists.
Since the first test suite fails at the first error, the second command will not be executed per your requirement to "run in sequence."
**Complete output from first command:** ``` ........................................................................ [ 1%] ........................................................................ Try 'handle-no-changes --help' for help.

The failure is in the test file at `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_handle_no_changes.py`, line 398, which references `--plan-id` option that appears to have been renamed as part of PR #9120 (Rename JSON output fields in exec scripts: plan → pr).
